lines.content: about: A flaw that affects most users and causes major problems for users. i.e., only
problems that make the product almost unusable for most users should have this label.
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.
about: A flaw that is unlikely to affect normal operations of the product. Appears
only in very rare situations and causes a minor inconvenience only.
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.
about: A flaw that causes occasional inconvenience to some users, but they can continue
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.
about: A flaw that is purely cosmetic and does not affect usage e.g., a typo/spacing/layout/color/font
issues in the docs or the UI that doesn't affect usage. Only cosmetic problems should
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.
about: An enhancement to an existing story
about: A big feature which can be broken down into smaller stories e.g., search
* CcaLedger — the orchestrator of the application.
* - Wires together Parser, Ui, CcaManager, ResidentManager
* - Calls Parser to get a Command, then calls command.execute()
* DO NOT add business logic here. This class only coordinates.
* Reads input → parses to Command → executes → repeats until exit.
* Instantiates CcaLedger and starts the run loop.
* Command — abstract base class for all commands.
* 1. Create a new file in the command/ package, e.g. AddCcaCommand.java
* 2. Extend Command:     public class AddCcaCommand extends Command
* 3. Implement execute() — put your logic here, calling manager methods
* 4. Store any parsed data (e.g. ccaName) as a constructor argument field
* 5. Do NOT print directly — always use ui.showMessage() or ui methods
* 6. Override isExit() ONLY in ExitCommand — leave it alone otherwise
* Override public void execute(CcaManager ccaManager, ResidentManager residentManager, Ui ui) {
* All real logic goes here — call manager methods, then call ui methods to print.
* @param ccaManager      manages the list of CCAs
* @param residentManager manages the list of Residents
* @param ui              used to display output — ONLY class that should print
public abstract void execute(CcaManager ccaManager, ResidentManager residentManager, Ui ui);
* Returns true if this command should end the application loop.
* Only ExitCommand should override this to return true.
* ExitCommand — signals the run loop to terminate.
* Triggered by the "bye" command word in Parser.
public void execute(CcaManager ccaManager, ResidentManager residentManager, Ui ui) {
// Nothing to do — CcaLedger will call ui.showGoodbye() after checking isExit()
* UnknownCommand — returned by Parser when input doesn't match any known command.
* Displays an error message. Teammates do not need to touch this file.
public void execute(CcaManager ccaManager, ResidentManager residentManager, Ui ui) {
ui.showError("Unknown command. Type 'help' for a list of commands.");
* Plain data model — fields only, no business logic.
* Add new fields (e.g. description, category) here if needed; logic goes in CcaManager.
* The display name of this CCA.
* @param name the name of the CCA, e.g. "Basketball"
* @return the CCA name as a string
* Plain data model — fields only, no business logic.
* Add new fields here if needed (e.g. room number); logic goes in ResidentManager.
private String ccaName;  // CCA this resident belongs to
private int points;      // participation points, defaults to 0
* @param name the resident's full name, e.g. "John Tan"
* @return the CCA name this resident is assigned to
* @param ccaName name of the CCA to assign this resident to
* @param points points to assign; call via ResidentManager, not directly from commands
* @return formatted string: "name | CCA: ccaName | Points: points"
return name + " | CCA: " + ccaName + " | Points: " + points;
* Parser — reads raw user input and returns the appropriate Command object.
* When you create a new Command class, add a case here to return it.
* 1. Add a new "case" entry below for your command word (e.g. "add-cca")
* 2. Parse any arguments from the input (e.g. extract the name after "n/")
* 3. Return a new instance of your Command, passing arguments to the constructor
* - Parser ONLY creates Command objects — it never executes them
* - Parser NEVER touches CcaManager, ResidentManager, or Ui
* - If input is invalid, return new UnknownCommand() — don't throw here
* Parses the user's input string and returns the matching Command.
* @param input the raw input string from the user
* @return the appropriate Command object to execute
// CCA commands — Veer adds cases here
// Resident commands — Aarav / Yi Yang add cases here
* Extracts the value after a prefix like "n/" from an argument string.
* @return the value after the prefix, or empty string if not found
public static String extractPrefix(String arguments, String prefix) {
* Ui — the ONLY class that reads input and prints output.
* When your command needs to display something, add a method here.
* NEVER use System.out.println anywhere outside this class.
* Add your own print methods at the bottom of this file.
* Reads a line of input from the user.
System.out.println(" Type a command to get started. Type 'bye' to exit.");
System.out.println(" Bye! See you next time.");
// CCA OUTPUT — Veer adds methods here
* Prints the full list of CCAs.
// TODO (Veer): implement display of CCA list
// RESIDENT OUTPUT — Rehaan adds methods here
* Prints the full list of residents.
// TODO (Rehaan): implement display of resident list
